title: val non-null hole (was jssm#755)
manager: A hole in the val (extended-state) type checking lets a value that should be non-null slip through as null, defeating the very guarantee the annotation exists to give. Fix so the non-null constraint actually holds. Ships as an early 6.0.x patch with the rest of the P1 val tail — small, real, and cheaper to close now than to carry the doubt into the contract era.
